seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with LangChain enables powerful code-aware agents that analyze repositories, generate fixes, and validate through sandbox execution. However, complex chains and agent loops introduce risks like hallucinated code, security vulnerabilities, and credential leaks that can lead to production failures or exploits. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a LangChain agent equipped with tools for repository analysis, code generation, sandbox execution, and test validation. User queries trigger a ReAct agent that retrieves relevant repo context via RAG, generates code fixes using a code-specialized LLM, executes in a sandbox, runs tests, and iterates until validation passes. Long conversations maintain state with memory buffers while tools handle git operations, AST parsing, and security scanning.

Key components include a vector store for repo embeddings, custom tools for sandbox exec and test running, and output parsers ensuring structured responses. The agent loop continues until convergence on working, secure code. Context management prevents token overflow in extended debugging sessions.

Verification occurs at critical gates: post-generation for syntax/security, post-execution for test passes, and final output for compliance.

risks.name: Hallucinated Incorrect Code
risks.description: Agent generates syntactically invalid or logically wrong code due to RAG failures or LLM drift
risks.mitigation: Wrap chain.invoke() with vex.verify() using code_execution and test validators

risks.name: Security Vulnerabilities
risks.description: Generated code contains injection flaws, unsafe deserialization, or command execution risks
risks.mitigation: Vex security scanner analyzes AST for OWASP Top 10 patterns and sandbox execution

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Agent accidentally includes API keys, passwords, or secrets from repo context in outputs
risks.mitigation: Vex credential detector scans all outputs against known patterns and repo secrets

risks.name: Infinite Tool Call Loops
risks.description: Agent gets stuck repeatedly calling sandbox/test tools without convergence
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors execution traces and enforces max iterations with early termination

implementation.title: Repository Context RAG Chain
implementation.description: Embed repo files and retrieve relevant context for the agent using LangChain's document loaders
implementation.code:
from langchain_community.document_loaders import DirectoryLoader
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS

# Load entire repo
loader = DirectoryLoader('./repo', glob="**/*.py", loader_cls=TextLoader)
docs = loader.load()

# Split and embed
splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=2000, chunk_overlap=200)
splits = splitter.split_documents(docs)
vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(splits, OpenAIEmbeddings())
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={'k': 8})

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Tool
implementation.description: Custom LangChain tool that executes generated code in isolated Docker sandbox
implementation.code:
from langchain.tools import BaseTool
from subprocess import run, PIPE
import docker

class SandboxExecutor(BaseTool):
    name = 'sandbox_execute'
    description = 'Execute Python code in secure sandbox. Input: code string'
    
    def _run(self, code: str) -> str:
        client = docker.from_env()
        container = client.containers.run(
            'python:3.11-slim',
            command=f'python -c "{code}"',
            mem_limit='256m',
            detach=True,
            remove=True
        )
        result = container.wait()
        logs = container.logs().decode()
        return f'Exit: {result}, Output: {logs}'

implementation.title: Agent with Code Tools
implementation.description: ReAct agent combining RAG, sandbox execution, and test validation tools
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_react_agent, AgentExecutor
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain import hub

llm = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4', temperature=0)

tools = [rag_retriever_tool, sandbox_executor, test_runner]

prompt = hub.pull('hwchase17/react')
agent = create_react_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, max_iterations=10, verbose=True)

result = agent_executor.invoke({
    'input': 'Fix bug in main.py: users report 500 errors on login'
})

implementation.title: TypeScript Agent Executor
implementation.description: LangChain.js equivalent with async tool execution for Node.js environments

vexIntegration.title: Verify Code Generation Output
vexIntegration.description: Wrap agent final output with Vex to validate syntax, security, and test execution
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

agent_result = agent_executor.invoke({'input': user_query})

verified = vex.verify(
    agent_result['output'],
    suite=[
        vex.code_execution(),
        vex.security_scan(owasp_top_10=True),
        vex.credential_detector(),
        vex.test_runner(pytest=True)
    ],
    timeout='30s'
)

if verified.passed:
    return verified.output
else:
    return f'Failed verification: {verified.errors}'

vexIntegration.title: Prevent Tool Call Loops
vexIntegration.description: Monitor agent execution traces for infinite loops and excessive iterations
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

with vex.span('agent_execution') as span:
    result = agent_executor.invoke({
        'input': query,
        'chat_history': memory.chat_memory.messages
    })
    
    # Vex auto-detects loops from trace patterns
    if not span.passed:
        raise Exception(f'Agent loop detected: {span.errors}')

# Extract verified output
verified_code = span.data['verified_output']
